title: hieracium murorum subsp. hyperlepideum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. hyperlepideum, also known as hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. hyperlepideum include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herb in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
